title: Suspicious
description: Master hub for everything citizen investigators flag as suspicious in the Charlie Kirk case, sorted by actor — law enforcement, FBI, Israel, US intel, defense counsel, TPUSA, the courts — plus UVU on 9/10, the five minutes, and cause of death.

title: The Acoustics Point South, Not to Losee (Claims)
description: An unnamed acoustics engineer, in an October 22, 2025 @ProjectConstitu video, places the shot South above the Teachers' balcony — conflicting with the site's own Canon audio reading of a rifle at ~120m. Rated THIN.

title: The ATF Fragment Shows Only Class Characteristics (Claims)
description: The ATF's September 17, 2025 lab report reportedly found the recovered jacket fragment shares only .30-caliber class characteristics with Robinson's Mauser 98 — neither identified nor excluded. Rated CONFIRMED, and overclaimed by both camps.

title: The Burnt Object as a RODE Transmitter (Claims)
description: Claims by @ProjectConstitu and @DiligentDenizen that the charred mass photographed in the SUV at an auto auction contains a melted RODE audio jack and a battery warning symbol. Rated THIN.

title: The Canon XA55 Audio and a Stage Detonation (Claims)
description: An unnamed analyst reads uncompressed Canon XA55 audio as three arrivals — Mach cone at +114ms, muzzle blast at +202ms placing a rifle ~120m out, and a louder detonation at the tent at +321ms. Rated EMERGING.

title: The Rick Cutler Hand-Device Claim (Claims)
description: Anonymous accounts read a dark object in Rick Cutler's hand as a gesture-control 'smart mouse' or CIA palm gun. Recorded only as a specimen of what circulates against a named private citizen. Rated THIN.

title: Electrocution as Trigger and Redundancy (Claims)
description: Three escalating anonymous claims that electricity triggered a charge through Kirk's body or killed him outright. No burn, arc mark, or current path was reported by anyone. Rated SPECULATIVE — the site's weakest strand.

title: No Blood Spray, No Exit Wound (Claims)
description: The most-repeated suspicion, raised by @HolonCitizen, Candace Owens and Chris Martenson: a ~2,900 ft-lb .30-06 should have left an exit wound and blood spray. Its 'knocked flying' premise is a physics error. Rated MODERATE.

title: Is the Front Neck Wound an Entrance or an Exit? (Claims)
description: Proponents including @stevendenoon argue the front neck wound is an exit, then contradict each other on whether the shot came from high behind or from inside the tent. No autopsy or wound exam produced. Rated THIN.

title: The Mic Placed Under, Not Over, the Shirt (Claims)
description: Research notes and followtheepicenter.com claim the RODE Wireless PRO transmitter was clasped under Kirk's FREEDOM shirt against his torso — the precondition every contact-device theory needs, and also routine AV practice. Rated SPECULATIVE.

title: The Snapped Necklace and the Residue Search (Claims)
description: The snapped chain read two incompatible ways: Chris Martenson's bullet cavitation versus @cletus_jethro's PETN micro-cone in the pendant, plus the reported federal search for the necklace. Rated SPECULATIVE.

title: Suspicious About Cause of Death
description: Eleven disputed items about how Charlie Kirk died — the ATF class-characteristics fragment, the energy mismatch, the shirt-before-blood sequence, the Canon audio, electrocution — and why the alternative mechanisms contradict each other.

title: The Shirt and Necklace Move Before the Blood (Claims)
description: Jon Bray's optical-flow work and whokilledck.com argue the shirt balloons and the necklace snaps several frames before blood appears — which hydrostatic cavitation also predicts, and 30fps cannot resolve. Rated EMERGING.

title: Baron Coleman Says Robinson Is in Grave Danger (Claims)
description: Attorney and radio host Baron Coleman said on a July 13, 2026 clip he is '100% convinced' Robinson was framed and is in grave danger of being killed. No threat evidence is offered. Rated SPECULATIVE.

title: Defense Acquiescence to the Sua Sponte Gag Order (Claims)
description: Anonymous posts ask why defense counsel never challenged Judge Tony Graf's sua sponte December 16, 2025 publicity order, which sweeps in witnesses and future counsel. Such orders are routine and usually protect the defendant. Rated THIN.

title: The Jailhouse Informant Transport Order (Claims)
description: An anonymous X account reads Judge Graf's transport order for inmate Jaxson Thomas Fox as proof a jailhouse snitch is being positioned to fabricate a Robinson confession. A ministerial docket entry proves none of that. Rated THIN.

title: Kent's Halted Probe as Defense Ammunition (Claims)
description: Daily Mail reporting says officials worried Joe Kent's NCTC foreign-interference inquiry could hand Robinson's lawyers a multi-suspect argument; commentators ask why counsel has not publicly demanded the material. Rated EMERGING.

title: The Miranda Timing Came From a Defense Filing (Claims)
description: Baron Coleman's 6:25 PM Miranda and 6:26 PM counsel-invocation timing traces to Bates-stamped exhibit 003996-R2, quoted in a defense camera-exclusion motion. Critics ask why no public suppression fight followed. Rated MODERATE.

title: Missing Surrender Video and Discovery Volume (Claims)
description: Baron Coleman says Washington County refused a newsroom's request for Robinson's surrender video and later called it deleted under a 30-day retention policy, while a March 27 discovery addendum suggests footage exists. Rated EMERGING.

title: Kathryn Nester and the Google Trends Spike (Claims)
description: An anonymous post reads a December 8-14, 2024 Google Trends spike as proof Kathryn Nester was pre-selected from Israel and 'handpicked by the prosecution.' Trends reports normalized interest, not IP addresses. Rated THIN.

title: Suspicious by Defense Attorneys
description: Nine questions raised about Tyler Robinson's capital defense — counsel selection, the unchallenged gag order, the Miranda exhibit, the jailhouse informant — against the record of the Nester, Burt and Novak team. Questions, not misconduct allegations.

title: Parents Reportedly Declined High-End Attorneys (Claims)
description: Two unsourced investigation-file entries: that Robinson's parents declined high-end attorneys, and that the prosecutor turned away volunteer lawyers. The claims contradict each other, and Utah prosecutors cannot pick defense counsel. Rated THIN.

title: Not-Guilty Plea Versus the Confession Narrative (Claims)
description: Candace Owens argues no sworn written confession exists and Robinson pleaded not guilty; Baron Coleman cites an unnamed relative. Critics ask why the defense has not publicly rebutted the confession narrative. Rated EMERGING.

title: Bomb Dogs Reportedly Kept From Parts of the Scene (Claims)
description: Candace Owens' secondhand assertion that the FBI kept bomb dogs away from certain places at UVU — a keystone of the rigged-microphone theory — against the file's own record of bomb dogs called at 11:17am. Rated THIN.

title: The Banned CBLA Bullet-Lead Test (Claims)
description: Anonymous investigators infer from the January 9 defense motion's 'metallurgical' testing proposal that the FBI is reviving Comparative Bullet Lead Analysis, retired in 2005 after a National Research Council review. Rated MODERATE.

title: FBI and ATF Reportedly Withholding Discovery (Claims)
description: A reported defense continuance motion claiming the ATF and FBI have withheld files held since September, including DNA data and chain-of-custody records for videos the prosecution planned to present. Rated EMERGING.

title: Enhanced Stairwell Photos Released After Custody (Claims)
description: Baron Coleman alleges the FBI released a second, enhanced set of stairwell suspect photos at 7:17 PM Central on September 11 — after, he argues, Robinson was already in custody and being Mirandized. Rated EMERGING.

title: Local Police Reportedly Told Not to Investigate (Claims)
description: Claims the FBI told local police not to contact witnesses or investigate, alongside Sgt. Jennifer Faumuina's reported testimony that DNA testing on recovered items stopped and she could not recall who ordered it. Rated EMERGING.

title: Suspicious by the FBI
description: Twelve items citizen investigators flag about the FBI specifically — the revived CBLA test, the re-search that found the rifle, Ryne Simmons' deleted 4K video, withheld discovery, and the halted Joe Kent foreign-nexus probe. Attributed claims.

title: Patel Reportedly Shut Down the Kent Probe (Claims)
description: Daily Mail reporting that FBI Director Kash Patel 'snuffed out' NCTC director Joe Kent's review of whether foreign powers had a nexus to the killing, amplified secondhand by Candace Owens and Russell Brand. Rated MODERATE.

title: The FBI Rav4 That Reportedly Flashed a Badge (Claims)
description: A single unsourced 'SUPER Strange events' entry: an FBI Rav4 drove past Tyler Robinson right after the shooting and its occupant flashed a badge when pulled over. No date, agent, or source cited. Rated THIN.

title: Feds Directed the Re-Search That Found the Rifle (Claims)
description: An unsourced file entry describing a four-step sequence: a local line search found no gun, K9s found no gun, federal agents arrived, and three junior officers were sent back and found the rifle. Rated EMERGING.

title: Selective 4K Release Versus the FBI Gag Claim (Claims)
description: Anonymous investigators argue TPUSA's October 14 birthday tribute video used crisp 4K clips and an overhead drone shot from September 10 — footage TPUSA had reportedly said could not be released on FBI advice. Rated THIN.

title: Ryne Simmons Reportedly Told to Delete His 4K Video (Claims)
description: Named witness Ryne Simmons says he filmed the shooting at 4K/60fps, sent it to the FBI, and was then told to delete his copy, with follow-up calls confirming; a parallel Skyler Baird claim is noted. Rated EMERGING.

title: A Surgeon Reportedly Blocked From Kirk's Room (Claims)
description: A one-clause, wholly unsourced list entry stating the FBI blocked a surgeon from going back into the room with Charlie Kirk — no name, hospital, time, agent, or record anywhere in the file. Rated THIN.

title: The SUV Destroyed and the ADL Floor-Sharing Claim (Claims)
description: Two low-confidence optics items: @iluminomoly's claim the transport SUV was chopped up and melted down, and an unsourced entry that an FBI Connecticut building shares a floor with the ADL. Rated THIN.

title: Farnsworth Already Filming From the Rock Pile (Claims)
description: Terryl Farnsworth reportedly filmed from a rock pile 27 seconds after the shot and said 'He's dead' at 12:24:36 — Candace Owens' foreknowledge framing weighed against the ordinary bystander explanation.

title: Three Minutes to the First Police Report (Claims)
description: The claim police audio carries the first report of the shooting only at 12:26, after the SUV left at 12:24 — an unlinked figure, and two and a half minutes to a radio channel is fast, not slow.

title: McCoy Reportedly Phones His Wife as the Shot Lands (Claims)
description: An unattributed reconstruction places TPUSA chief of staff Mikey McCoy calling his wife Elizabeth at 12:23:30, the same second as the shot; no carrier record, log, or screenshot supports the timestamp.

title: McCoy Alone in Frame, Neff Nowhere (Claims)
description: Erika Kirk says Mikey McCoy called her at 12:23 and Blake Neff says he witnessed it, yet at 12:24:02 McCoy is reportedly alone in frame — framed as a timeline question, not an accusation.

title: The N1098L Low Pass at 12:24 (Claims)
description: N1098L, an aircraft tied to the Army HADES program, reportedly made a 335-foot low pass near UVU at 12:24 — thirty seconds after the shot, too fast for any reaction, with the file's own figures conflicting.

title: Suspicious Within Five Minutes
description: Chronological hub for the ten minutes from 12:18:30 to 12:28:30 PM MT on September 10, 2025 — ten flagged items from the 12:22 roof run to the 12:26 first police report.

title: The Rifle Wrapped, Placed on the Roof, the Descent (Claims)
description: A compiled timeline says the rifle was wrapped in a dark towel and set on the Losee Center roof at 12:24, yet the Mauser was recovered in the woods — the tension and how it resolves.

title: The 12:22 Roof Run and the Vanishing Limp (Claims)
description: The claim the roof figure limped all morning then ran west across the Losee Center roof at 12:22 with the limp no longer noticeable — unsourced, resting on an observer impression.

title: The Claim the Runner Started Before the Shot (Claims)
description: The claim that back-extrapolating the rooftop runner puts his start at 12:23:28, two seconds before the shot, making it 'literally impossible' — arithmetic that wrongly assumes constant velocity from a standing start.

title: The Shirt Pulled at the Instant of the Shot (Claims)

title: CornerShot Technology (Israeli Hardware Claims)

title: Foreign Phones at UVU (Sept 10, 2025)

title: More Reasons Tyler Is Not the Assassin

title: Tyler Robinson Not Assassin

title: .30-06 Bullet Did Not Kill Charlie

title: Social Media Analysis
